title: Terminal Dogma Essays on Neon Genesis Evangelion
display_title: Terminal Dogma Essays on Neon Genesis Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Terminal Dogma: Essays on Neon Genesis Evangelion             Quote Post by AuthenticM » Mon Oct 02, 2006 1:15 pm The following is quoted from ANN: Upcoming Volume Solicits Fans and Academics Alike for EssaysBrian Ruh, author of Stray Dog of Anime: The Films of Mamoru Oshii and the maintainer of Anime Research has announced an upcoming tome of essays dealing with Neon Genesis Evangelion, tentatively titled Terminal Dogma: Essays on Neon Genesis Evangelion. The volume, edited and compiled by Brian Ruh, will focus not only on the show itself, but also how Evangelions has been discussed, marketed, and understood. Another purpose of the volume is to foster dialogue between academics studying anime and anime fans. As such, Ruh is requesting essay submissions not only from academics, but from anime fans as well. Aspects covered by the chapters could include (but are certainly not limited to): Psychoanalysis and Evangelion Narrative structures of the TV series / films The reception of Evangelion (both in Japan and in other countries) Evangelion on DVD The merchandising of Evangelion Evangelion manga Evangelion video games The role of Evangelion in anime canon formation Evangelion in the context of Hideaki Annoâ€™s / Gainaxâ€™s other works Evangelionâ€™s references to previous anime Music in Evangelion Evangelion fanfiction / doujinshi Animators / animation techniques in Evangelion Evangelion fandom / websites / shrines Language use / translating Evangelion Final essays will be around 5000 words. Please send proposals of 500-700 words or completed essays as well as a brief CV / personal bio to the editor at evabook[at]gmail.com Deadline: December 31, 2006 Interesting. I think some of us here could very well contribute to this.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Oct 02, 2006 1:43 pm For some reason, the concept of writing essays for a tome edited by some guy I've never heard of, slated for actual Dead Tree publication, and intended to have academic applications terrifies me. More importantly, in terms of curriculum vitae , I'm an unqualified nobody with no impressive pieces of paper to wave around.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 02, 2006 1:57 pm

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Oct 02, 2006 3:42 pm "Whoa", indeed. Can't you see McCoy's extensive essay on Psychoanalysis in NGE already? I sure can. And let's not forget the requisite dose of Dennis Redmond. Carl better have something in this thing. I want to see at least one familiar name. (And yes -- I'm probably feeling bitter about the fact that I only have an Associate's.)

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Oct 02, 2006 6:08 pm Reichu wrote: (And yes -- I'm probably feeling bitter about the fact that I only have an Associate's.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Oct 02, 2006 6:14 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: If it weren't for that deadline I'd probably be interested, but I've got to be realistic.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 02, 2006 9:33 pm Reichu wrote: Cuz I honestly don't know what I'd write about, even if I'm considered eligible.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Mon Oct 02, 2006 9:51 pm Maybe I should send my NERV did WTC theory to this. I bet I'll get it published, for it's the TRUTH!

[]:             Quote Post by AuthenticM » Mon Oct 02, 2006 10:35 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Reichu wrote: Cuz I honestly don't know what I'd write about, even if I'm considered eligible.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 02, 2006 10:39 pm AuthenticM wrote: You guys would contribute a lot if you would just take the time. If you don't however, you'll regret it forever. So make some coffee and on with the show!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Oct 02, 2006 10:54 pm AuthenticM wrote: She is, and so are you.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Tue Oct 03, 2006 12:06 am Both of you are more than qualified to submit something, and you're both not bad at writing, from what I've seen. Heck, I might have to think up a topic myself. Oh, and I'll volunteer my services as an editor for anyone interested. I was a history major in college, so I have some experience with academic papers.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Oct 03, 2006 2:22 pm Reichu wrote: Hexon.Arq wrote: If it weren't for that deadline I'd probably be interested, but I've got to be realistic.

[]:             Quote Post by Otaprince » Sat Oct 07, 2006 5:13 pm This sounds pretty interesting-- I'm tinkering with a submission or two. Although I've had some novel ideas about Eva, I'm not sure any of them are substantial and interesting enough to sustain even a short article, but hey, we'll see. Perhaps Eva within the context of Gainax works that came before and after? Hmm... Evamonkey and Reichu, if you are struggling for a topic, here's one that each or both of you could answer pretty well: What the f*** happens in Evangelion? Not the metaphors, not the debates about meaning, but simply what events happen in what order. Like, I run a website about Gainax, have seen Eva a dozen times, consider myself a huge fan of the show but have no idea what the freaking black moon is. It seems like you guys have hammered this out on the EM forums over the past few years, but so far none of that is in one place, one document. It would be nice to have one complete, authoritative text on what happens when, and where are there areas of debate that will never be resolved? Lance of Longinus, Adam, Lilith, Impacts, etc. The Eva Otaku FAQ is great in terms of the major events, but it seems like there's a lot of small details that aren't in there.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Oct 07, 2006 7:02 pm Otaprince wrote: Evamonkey ... if you are struggling for a topic...

[]:             Quote Post by Ion » Mon Feb 05, 2007 4:58 am It's February 5 already. There can I find this essay?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Feb 05, 2007 3:24 pm I agree writing with about just what the fuck happens in Eva. Understanding all the little details is a good thing. ^^; Though it's way too late for this book, that's why there's the internet, right?

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Sun Feb 11, 2007 5:08 am Evangelion gets THIS far? Wow.... :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Mon Apr 20, 2009 2:21 pm Major bump because I can't find info. 1. Did anyone end up submitting anything? 2. What came of the project (was a book produced)?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Apr 20, 2009 6:43 pm I'm going to have images of my cake published in the upcoming Raising Project manga. Does that "qualify" me?
